integration_map.change: Declare BladeLoadCorrFactor as a ReKi scalar in the AD_ParameterType section so the Registry code-generator emits the new field in AD_ParameterType
Regenerated file: after Registry update, re-run the Registry tool so AD_ParameterType gains the BladeLoadCorrFactor field; do not hand-edit
Add BladeLoadCorrFactor keyword in ReadPrimaryFile / input-parsing routines so the parameter is read from AeroDyn's own input deck
SetParameters: assign p%BladeLoadCorrFactor from parsed InputFileData; add validation in ValidateInputData (line 4305)
SetOutputsFromBEMT: preferred injection point -- multiply blade-element force/moment values by p%BladeLoadCorrFactor when transferring BEMT results to the blade mesh, keeping RotCalcOutput clean

RotCalcOutput: alternative injection point -- apply p%BladeLoadCorrFactor to y%BladeLoad mesh after SetOutputsFromBEMT returns; choose exactly one of this or the SetOutputsFromBEMT site to avoid double-application

BEMT_CalcOutput (deepest blade-element site): if the correction must influence induction or aerodynamic coefficients before output assembly, pass BladeLoadCorrFactor into BEMT_ParameterType and apply it here; requires parallel Registry/Types changes for BEMT
FAST_CalcOutput: no structural changes required if BladeLoadCorrFactor stays within AD_ParameterType (opaque to the glue layer); only modify if a FAST-level override mechanism is needed

FAST_InitializeAll: passes AeroDyn InitInput to AD_Init; add BladeLoadCorrFactor to that structure only if the parameter must be settable from the FAST primary input file rather than AeroDyn's own input deck
